title: Complex Network Measures of Brain Connectivity: Uses and Interpretations
abstract: Brain connectivity datasets comprise networks of brain regions connected by structural and/or functional links. Complex network analysis—a new multidisciplinary approach to the study of complex systems—aims to characterize these networks with a small number of neurobiologically meaningful and easily computable measures. This article provides a comprehensive overview of these measures and discusses their uses and interpretations.
notes: Already in existing 102 papers. Defines standard graph measures for brain networks; extremely highly cited.
